metadata.seed: Well, I just get very anxious. I don't want to leave him in the room without me. I want to be right there. I'm not used to leaving him alone with other people at all. And I know I get very anxious in the waiting area, and I pace and back and forth and wonder what's going on in the room and listening for anything that might be going on. So, yeah, I just have a lot of anxiety about being separated from him, even for that short time.

patient.query: Well, I just get very anxious. I don't want to leave him in the room without me. I want to be right there. I'm not used to leaving him alone with other people at all. And I know I get very anxious in the waiting area, and I pace and back and forth and wonder what's going on in the room and listening for anything that might be going on. So, yeah, I just have a lot of anxiety about being separated from him, even for that short time.

triggers: leaving him alone with other people
automatic_thoughts: I don't want to leave him in the room without me
I wonder what's going on in the room
I might miss something happening

entailment: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)
neutral: Fear of not waking from general anesthetic (finding)

llm_response.response: I hear how anxious you feel when you have to leave him alone. What thoughts come up when you imagine him being in the room without you? Have you noticed any times when you were apart and things turned out differently than you feared?

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, it gets really overwhelming sometimes. I just keep thinking that something bad could happen, like he might get hurt or need me and I won't be there. But I guess there have been times when I left and he was fine... It's just hard to remember those moments when the worry kicks in.

automatic_thoughts: something bad could happen
he might get hurt
I won't be there

entailment: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the worry can feel overwhelming, especially when you think about him needing you. What evidence do you have that something bad will happen? What evidence do you have that it won't?

patient.query: I know, logically, that he usually does fine when I'm not around. I mean, he's a grown man, right? But in those moments, all I can think about is the "what ifs"—what if he has an accident or something happens while I'm not there? It's like I can't shake off this heavy feeling... it's just so much louder than any reassurance.

automatic_thoughts: what if he has an accident
what if something happens while I'm not there
behaviors: can't shake off this heavy feeling

neutral: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)
Anxiety about not coping with parenthood (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those “what if” thoughts are really heavy for you. What evidence do you have that something might happen when he’s alone? Have there been times when he was alone and nothing went wrong? How might you view the situation if you considered those times?

patient.query: Yeah, they really are heavy. I mean, I know he’s been fine alone before, but... it’s like those times just get overshadowed by the fear. I can’t help but replay all the possible scenarios in my head, even if they don’t make sense. It's hard to focus on the moments when everything went okay because the fear just feels so much more real.

automatic_thoughts: replay all the possible scenarios
those times just get overshadowed by the fear
the fear just feels so much more real

neutral: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the fear is really pulling you in, even when you know the situation has been okay before. What evidence do you have that the fear is more real than the times when everything went smoothly? Could there be another way to view those moments that might help balance the intensity of the fear?

patient.query: It really is heavy sometimes. I know he's been fine alone before, but those times just seem to fade away when the fear kicks in. It’s like my mind starts racing with all the worst-case scenarios, and I can’t help but get lost in them. The fear feels so much more real than any reassurance I try to remind myself of.

automatic_thoughts: my mind starts racing with all the worst-case scenarios
those times just seem to fade away

neutral: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the heaviness you’re feeling is really intense, especially when those fears surface. It’s understandable that the worst‑case thoughts feel more vivid than any reassurance you try to hold onto. You’re not alone in noticing how powerful those fears can be.

patient.query: It really is heavy sometimes. I know he’s been fine alone before, but those moments just fade away when the fear kicks in. My mind races with all the worst-case scenarios, and I can’t help but get lost in them. The fear feels so much more real than any reassurance I tell myself.

automatic_thoughts: he's not fine alone
I can't handle this
something bad will happen

neutral: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the fear feels very real and overwhelming. What evidence do you have that the worst‑case scenarios will actually happen? Are there moments when you’ve seen things work out differently than you expect?

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely feels overwhelming. I mean, I guess I can think of times when things went fine, like the last time I left him alone for a bit. He didn’t need me, and everything went okay. But even remembering that is tough because the anxiety just drowns it out. It’s like I can’t hold on to those moments when the worry feels so consuming.

automatic_thoughts: the anxiety drowns out positive memories
I can't hold on to good moments

neutral: Anxiety about not coping with parenthood (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the anxiety is so intense that even recalling a calm moment feels hard. It can be frustrating when the good memories feel drowned out. What might help you notice those moments more clearly when they arise?

patient.query: Yeah, it really can be frustrating. I don’t know, maybe if I tried to focus on them more when they happen? But when I'm in that anxious space, everything just feels so chaotic. It's like I know I should remember those good moments, but they're just... overshadowed by the worry. I guess I just wish there was a way to make the fear quieter so I could feel more present in those calmer times.

automatic_thoughts: I should remember those good moments
The fear is overshadowing everything
I wish the fear was quieter
behaviors: try to focus on good moments

llm_response.response: Can you describe what happens when you try to bring those good moments to mind? What signs tell you that the worry is taking over? Are there any moments, even brief, where you notice the calmness slipping back in?

patient.query: It’s really frustrating. I mean, I know I should focus on those good moments, but when the anxiety hits, everything feels chaotic. I want to remember the times he was fine without me, but the worry just drowns them out. I wish the fear would quiet down so I could feel more present during those calmer times.

automatic_thoughts: I should focus on those good moments
Everything feels chaotic
The worry drowns out good memories
I wish the fear would quiet down

neutral: Anxiety about not coping with parenthood (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re feeling really frustrated when anxiety takes over and makes it hard to focus on the good moments. What evidence do you have that trying to focus on those moments actually helps you feel calmer? Could there be other ways to bring those positive memories to mind when the anxiety rises?

patient.query: It’s really frustrating. I know I should focus on those good moments, but when the anxiety hits, everything feels chaotic. I want to remember the times he was fine without me, but the worry just drowns them out. I wish the fear would quiet down so I could feel more present during those calmer times.

automatic_thoughts: I should focus on those good moments
Everything feels chaotic
The worry drowns out good memories
I wish the fear would quiet down
